methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $50M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $150M - $500M.

data.narrative:
Swift Beat is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Eric Schmidt that has raised $50M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peer companies: 2 public peers (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, Anduril Industries, and Saronic.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $50M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implying a $100M valuation. Peer-median analysis isolates 3 same-sector, same-stage comparables with valuations of $500M (Stark Defence), $300M (Vatn Systems), and $150M (Ares Industries), yielding a median of $300M and a range of $150M–$500M. The peer set reflects the fragmented structure of frontier defense technology, where valuations at this stage vary considerably.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $85M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $200M. The wide band reflects material uncertainty around which peer cohort most accurately reflects Swift Beat's market position and risk profile. Stage-progression and peer methodology diverge by $200M, suggesting investors should stress-test assumptions about comparable growth trajectories and market timing in the defense sector before anchoring to any point estimate.
